title: PostgreSQL client + migration runner
acceptance_criteria: src/db/client.ts exports: a Pool instance (pg) created from DATABASE_URL env var; a typed query helper query<T>(sql, params?) returning Promise<T[]>; a queryOne<T>(sql, params?) returning Promise<T | null>; a withTransaction<T>(fn) helper that wraps a callback in BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK

src/db/migrate.ts: reads all *.sql files from src/db/migrations/ in ascending lexicographic order; creates schema_migrations table if absent (id SERIAL, filename TEXT UNIQUE, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()); skips already-applied migrations; applies each in a transaction where possible; retries connection up to 3 times with 2s/4s/8s backoff on connection failure

TimescaleDB extension check: before running any migrations, execute SELECT installed_version FROM pg_extension WHERE extname='timescaledb'; if no row returned, exit with process.exit(1) and print: 'ERROR: TimescaleDB extension not found. Use image timescale/timescaledb:latest-pg16, not postgres:16-alpine.'
Migration idempotency: running bun run migrate twice against the same database produces identical state (no duplicate errors, no re-application of already-applied migrations)
All exports are named exports (no default exports)
pg NUMERIC columns configured to return as strings via pg.types.setTypeParser for OID 1700 — this prevents floating-point coercion of decimal values
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
